title: The Question
script:
Hi, this is Henry from Typework.
Before we start, let's take a look at something.
Here are two companies — same industry, same starting revenue. Five years later, they look very different.
Now, if you're an investor, you'd definitely bet on the first one.
But what made the difference between them?

title: The Answer
script:
The answer is clear: Company A is built on software: more income, more revenue.
Company B is built on headcount: more income, only more headcounts.
We see Company A went from 2 million to 50 millions. Only 10 people, serving 5000 clients. Margins get better every quarter.
And Company B? 85 people with only 200 clients. Margins shrink every year.
They starting at the same with totally different outcome.
Why?

title: The Problem
script:
Because every dollar of new revenue costs Company B another dollar of labor.
They hire to grow, and they hire to keep up, growing but breaking. Revenue stays flat, costs keep going up, and the margin just collapses.
It's the same three things killing them — people, actions, decisions — all manual, all slow, none of it scales.
And this isn't just a Company B problem.
This is why 95% of SMEs stay below 10 million. They can't outgrow their own operating cost.

title: The Transformation
script:
So what if Company B could fly — just like Company A?
With a one-stop platform. Same business, but AI agents do the rest.
We turn Company B into Company A — without hiring a single engineer.

title: Introducing Typework
script:
Introducing Typework. We make every business scalable.
One platform to launch your Full-stack company.

How big is the market? 95% of all businesses in the world are SMEs — and they all share the same problem.
So who do we go after first?

Owners whose P&L has been declining. They've had their peak — they've made real money before — they know what the good days look like.
But now costs keep rising and margins keep shrinking.
We go straight to the boss. This is a top-down decision. The boss feels the pain, the boss makes the call.

title: Meet Our First Customer
script:
Let's meet Mark. He runs a yoga studio in Hong Kong with 10 coaches and employees on his team.
What's his entire operating system? WhatsApp, Instagram, Excel, and his own brain.

In the morning, Mark's phone starts ringing nonstop.
Users from different apps are reaching out to him: some asking questions, some renewing subscriptions, some complaining about coaches, and others just chatting.
After handling everything, Mark has to open his massive Excel file — with tons of columns and sheets — to manage today's bookings.
There are typos, duplicated rows, incorrect times, and sometimes changes aren't even saved.

Earlier, we said people and actions are killers. Now let's talk about decisions.
Any unexpected situation can instantly create a huge amount of extra work for Mark.
In the afternoon, one coach suddenly asks to take leave. So Mark has to reopen that same messy Excel again, reschedule, and coordinate replacements for clients.
Then he jumps back into his messaging apps — only to realize he can't even find some of the conversations anymore.

Life is hard — and tomorrow will be the same for Mark.
No system. No automation. No way out. That's who we're building for.

title: What Typework Does
script:
So how does Typework actually solve this?
Think of Typework as your own CIO.
Today, if you're an SME trying to scale, somebody will tell you: get a CRM, set up an ERP, add a POS system, implement HR tools — and you'll probably still need to hire an engineering team.
But these systems are expensive, inflexible, and the worst part? They don't talk to each other.
With Typework, you don't need any of that. Just upload your documents, describe your business — and your AI agent handles the rest.
More importantly, your agent connects your business to a network where businesses agents collaborate naturally, unlocking new opportunities.

title: The Shift + Opportunity
script:
And once your Agent on the network, here's what happens.
Traditional supply chains are linear — five businesses, four connections.
On Typework, those same five businesses create fifteen connections. And it compounds fast.
Every new business that joins makes the whole network more valuable.

title: Synergy: Opportunity Sharing
script:
Let me show you how this actually works.
Our client, IX Catering uploads their docs, Typework builds a booking agent automatically.
Customer books a table, mentions it's his wife's birthday.
The agent confirms — and because it's connected to the network — it pulls in flowers from Florist B and a cake from Cake Shop C.
With one booking, three business opportunity was created with zero human coordination.

title: Synergy: Smart Bundling
script:
Another example: Weddings — one booking activates nine vendor agents in minutes.
Average booking value goes up for 2400 dollars, just from proactive vendor bundling.
Same pattern everywhere. One trigger, multiple businesses, real dollars.

title: Competitive Landscape
script:
Where do we sit?
Look at the market today. On one side — AI agent tools, like Zapier. They automate tasks… but only inside one business.
Then marketplaces such as Thumbtack. They connect buyers and sellers… but there's no intelligence.
And legacy SaaS like Odoo. They digitize operations… but everything stays siloed.
So everyone stops there. We start here. Typework combines three things: AI agents, a business network, and zero code platform.

title: GTM Strategy
script:
So how do we go to market? We have four growth engines that feed into each other like a flywheel.
First, Industry AI Templates. Our solution engineers build the first templates with real customers. Then AI learns the best practices, distills the skills, and scales them across verticals. Each template becomes its own campaign page for customer acquisition.
Second, Ecosystem Integrations. We meet users where they already are — WhatsApp, Lark, Slack, Google Business. They experience Typework's core value with zero friction, then convert to the full platform.
Third, Agent Network Effects. Once we hit critical mass in a city, the network grows organically. Businesses start inviting their own supply chain. Want more business opportunities? You have to be on Typework.
And fourth, Ecosystem GTM Partners. Think co-working spaces, franchise networks, industry associations. One partnership can onboard 50 to 200 businesses at once.
Each engine feeds the next. Together, they create compounding growth that's very hard to replicate.

title: Team
script:
Quick introduction on the team. James and I are here at Antler Disrupt.
We've worked together for six years — we know how to ship.
Our CTO Fei Long built PyGWalker, an open-source project with over 15,000 GitHub stars — he knows how to build products people actually use.
We've built and scaled products before. This time we're building one together.

title: Traction
script:
We're not just building — we're already in market.
Around 20 companies in our pipeline. Multiple customers are live and deploying pilots right now.
And we've got early enterprise traction — a Hong Kong public sector organization with over 1,500 employees.
The demand is real, and it's inbound.

title: Closing
script:
Thank you. We're Typework.
We make every business scalable.
Let's talk.
